label: Sparse lexical retrieval

node.label: Sparse lexical retrieval
node.definition: Sparse lexical retrieval ranks documents from token overlap or learned term weights, typically through inverted indexes and relevance scoring such as BM25.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Sparse lexical retrieval is modeled as depending on External retrieval & ranking, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units and feeding downstream nodes such as Hybrid retrieval & reranking, External databases, APIs & live tools.
examples: atlas downstream link to Hybrid retrieval & reranking
atlas downstream link to External databases, APIs & live tools
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Sparse lexical retrieval has a `uses` dependency on External retrieval & ranking because External retrieval & ranking is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Sparse lexical retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.
In this atlas graph, Sparse lexical retrieval has a `uses` dependency on Databases & information retrieval because Databases & information retrieval is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
